description: city in Duval County, and largest city in State of Florida, United States
description_wiki: Jacksonville (US:  , JAK-sən-vil; colloquially nicknamed Jax) is the most populous city proper in the U.S. state of Florida, located on the Atlantic coast of northeastern Florida.
Amaliada (Greek: Αμαλιάδα, Katharevousa: Ἀμαλιάς, Amaliás) is a town and a former municipality in northwestern Elis, in the West Greece Region of Greece.

description: large peninsula and geographic region in southern Greece; named after the Greek mythological hero Pelops
description_wiki: The Peloponnese, Peloponnesus, or Morea, is a peninsula and geographic region in Southern Greece, and the southernmost region of the Balkans.

description: reputed author of the Iliad and the Odyssey
description_wiki: Homer (; Ancient Greek: Ὅμηρος [hómɛːros], Hómēros; possibly born c. the 8th century BC) was an ancient Greek poet who is widely credited as the author of the Iliad and the Odyssey, two epic poems that are foundational works of ancient Greek literature.

description: Athenian statesman, orator and general (c. 495 – 429 BC)
description_wiki:
Pericles ( ; Ancient Greek: Περικλῆς; c. 495–429 BC) was a Greek statesman 
and general during the Golden Age of Athens.

description: Greek philosopher and mathematician (c. 570 – c. 495 BC)
description_wiki: Pythagoras of Samos (Ancient Greek: Πυθαγόρας; c. 570 – c. 495 BC) was an ancient Ionian Greek philosopher, polymath, and the eponymous founder of Pythagoreanism.

description: city and port of Attica region, Greece
description_wiki: Piraeus ( py-REE-əs, pirr-AY-əs; Greek: Πειραιάς Peiraiás [pireˈas]; Ancient Greek and Katharevousa: Πειραιεύς Peiraieús, Ancient: [peːrai̯eús], Katharevousa: [pireˈefs]) is a port city within the Athens urban area ("Greater Athens"), in the Attica region of Greece.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.
Sparta was a prominent city-state in Laconia in ancient Greece.

surface_forms: the ancient Greek you know like the time of Christ
description_wiki: Greek (Modern Greek: ελληνικά, romanized: elliniká [eliniˈka] ; Ancient Greek: ἑλληνική, romanized: hellēnikḗ [helːɛːnikɛ́ː]) is an Indo-European language, constituting an independent Hellenic branch within the Indo-European language family.
